text:
I found The Evewell disorganised, chaotic, and worryingly unresponsive. Nurses frequently sent urgent instructions at the end of the day, making it impossible to clarify errors or ask questions. On one occasion, I was prescribed hormone patches they already knew I was allergic to. I had to use one anyway, and despite informing them of the reaction I can have, no one followed up or checked if I was ok.

During another appointment, I was physically hurt during a scan. I reported significant pain and bleeding, and raised concerns that the sonographer lacked adequate training. There was no clinical follow-up and no safeguarding response. I really felt I was bothering them when trying to follow up about it.

Despite their polished web presence, it really seemed that they are overstretched, under-coordinated, and have lost sight of patient safety. The overall culture felt rushed, dismissive, and complacent - unacceptable at this price point, and unacceptable full stop.

We were patients at Evewell in 2024 for private fertility investigations.

In November 2025, our daughter was born sleeping. Post mortem results confirmed …

Sadly had a bad experience with this clinic. The consultant we saw was very nice and understanding but apart from that this clinic for me was such a let down! The unnecessary delays for simple things like blood tests took weeks. We had to chase the clinic for several results. We were lied to about that they hadn’t received them yet, even though they had told us they’d received the results a few weeks prior but it was a letter from the consultant they were waiting for.

The reception staff I experienced weren’t very professional. One time I had an appointment, checked in with them and I was just passed a card machine with a payment on it to pay, without a word. Several times I was there I heard unprofessional conversations that were happening between staff around patients. Dates for appointments I had were changed several times by them/appointment booked on days I said I was not available so had to chase them to correct it. Basic things that you’d expect in any customer service environment that aren’t difficult, but especially considering it’s a fertility climic makes it even worse!
For such an advertised company I was expecting more. As they messed up/had such long delays several times with such easy things as blood tests we didn’t trust them with anything more so cut our losses and have found a clinic which is so above and beyond this one. The communication in comparison has been amazing and we completely trust our new clinic. Night and day comparison!

I would advise anyone if they don’t have a good feeling from this clinic/have issues with them to do the same as something so personal, expensive and precious you should trust the clinic you’re using wholeheartedly!

I have seen other bad reviews on this clinic appear and then disappear so, I wouldn’t be surprised if this one sadly disappears too!

Clearly some people have a good experience with this clinic however we did not. We tried to start the process in February this year and got some tests done and then had a rushed consultation with the Doctor who came across as dismissive and disinterested. The clinic required us to get some additional medical history information before they could offer treatment which I was able to do in March. Despite me then emailing Evewell multiple times asking what the next steps were and whether treatment could go ahead after sending them the requested information, they just never responded to any communication about treatment. This resulted in us being strung along with no responses for months before we eventually cut our losses and went to a different clinic in May which has been fantastic. The only communication Evewell responded to was emails where I requested copies of the tests I did with them so that I could use them elsewhere. It is frustrating because we of course paid for me and my partners tests and the consultation which are expensive, and we wouldn’t have done this at Evewell if we knew we would be going elsewhere for treatment due to their lack of communication.

Tried to cancel my appointment with this clinic.
Phone is never answered or is dropped. The admin staff got my date of birth wrong and they couldn’t even find my appointment and kept asking if I was calling the right clinic despite me telling them over and over again.
Turns out they had my date of birth incorrect.

I’m glad I’ve decided to go to another clinic as others have also had admin problems. This clinic really needs to sort out admin. Shame.

My experience at the Evewell overall was poor. We had testing and two unnecessary rounds of treatment, which was expensive, stressful and emotionally draining. Unfortunately I had endometriosis which was overlooked, even though I asked at two separate consultations if it could be endometriosis. After seeking out a specialist myself and having surgery, I fell pregnant naturally with my son. I sent an email to the doctor to explain what happened to try to help other couples from having unnessary treatment, which they didn't acknowlegde.

The communication of this place was absolutely abysmal. It started right at the very beginning when we registered with the clinic - incorrect names and DOB on forms and ‘missed’ emails leading to a delay in booking an appointment. Once we …

Disappointing experience. Many of the doctors that work there are not on the Specialist Register.  The admin staff take ages to return emails, the phone is never answered and voicemails are not returned.  I booked a 'starter phone consult' and this was not attended by Evewell.  As it stands the clinic haven't contacted me and henceforth I haven't been able to make an appointment.  I have submitted a formal complaint to them and this has not been responded to.

Admin staff are not informed about the process and I was not able to speak to anyone clinical to gather more insight.

Reading reviews it appears there are many others that have suffered from the abysmal admin support.

Very terrible customer service.

If i could give The Evewell a 0 star i would. Three different telephone/video appointments were made (and confirmed in writing) and on all occasions the calls had not been correctly scheduled and the clinic failed to call. To top it off, due to this lack of competency, the clinic promised a refund - i am still waiting for this refund. How a clinic that specialises in fertility care can be careless, i have no idea. I would recommend avoiding at all costs. The lack of care with clients emotional and financial wellbeing is really shameful.

3 years later and I still have ptsd from this place. Money hungry people. Will push random things you don’t need on you I.E in my case icsi, just  for extra money no actual science behind it. Unsuccessful, so many extra costs, have to do paperwork twice as poor admin loses them. There is no bespoke care. You will see whichever doctor or nurse that’s available even to my transfer day it was switched last minute!! They also ran very late and with a full bladder that was hell. Ultimately thousands and thousands down the drain. I am not angry because the transfer failed as that is the absolute least of my concerns with this awful place. Please, please avoid!!!!!!!!! They have my thousands on pounds please do not let them have yours they prey on the vulnerable and desperate.I strongly believe the positive results, perhaps not all, but many are fake. There was a time when there were non stop negative reviews now suddenly it’s glowing? Yeah right.
Click on ‘ negative reviews’ and see just how many are upvoted. That is a true reflection of this awful incompetent place.
I genuinely feel sick to my stomach when I think of this place.

I was quite unhappy when my doctor abruptly departed the clinic in the middle of my treatment. I had only gotten an impersonal email as a form of communication. Following this, I saw multiple doctors, and communication was terrible. They had to repeat blood tests, which was incredibly costly. After a disappointing first round of IVF, I decided to try again at a different clinic, and I'm so glad I did!

Was a lot more expensive than quoted as there were expensive add-ons that we were not told about  (ie: 2 minute scans at £200 a time, medication at £500 at time!). After 2 failed attempts our money run out and we never heard a thing from them - not even a courtesy call. I guess it's all about the money. The procedure itself was fine and the clinic was nice. If you are wealthy and money is not an issue then this clinic may be fine for you.

The worst clinic I've ever seen! Extremely rude! The doctor made me
feel extremely uncomfortable every time, only cared about money. Was unorganised too as they lost my blood results which was inconvenient. The first time you go for your consultation, they're nice to you. Once they get your money, they don't care anymore. Every time you go there, they charge you ridiculous money for just blood tests which they don't tell you about before hand. IVF treatment requires professionals who understand patient's needs fully, deeply care about them and are compassionate as the whole process can be extremely mentally draining.

Thoroughly unimpressed. No direct communication with my doctor which was so frustrating. My doctor also didn’t do any scans himself so relied on communication which was awful. The timings were missed with my ovulation and I had to cancel my cycle. Don’t bother with the Evewell!! Asked for a refund but was refused, awful.

The clinic might be very good but I am very disappointed by the accessibility of its services. I have sent out multiple emails and also tried to book an appointment via telephone call but I was always asked to wait for someone to get back …

Very sad that I started my treatment here!
Long waiting! Never able to see the main doctor. Half of the time the phone services are not available.
For the price I’m paying I would expect to have better service.
Would love to us this clinic but they never answer their phone or return your voicemails. Trying to get any help is very very hard!

We started our initial conversation at the Evewell one year ago. The communication was so bad that we wasted a lot of time on waiting (usually 3-5 weeks for a doctor’s letter after consultation, 4-5 weeks for a blood test result, and months …

I decided on the Evewell because I was looking for a clinic that would offer one to one care. As they are one of the most expensive clinics and they promised this I had high hopes. After my first appointment I immediately bounced to another doctor. I ended up seeing several doctors and it was very frustrating as they weren’t communicating with each other. They even ordered double labs at my expense and gave conflicting recommendations. The PAs I dealt with were very disorganised and overall it was really not the experience I was hoping for. I stopped my treatment at Evewell as I just didn’t feel comfortable. So glad I made that decision because I found a clinic that is much better and way less expensive.

We had an unsuccessful round of IVF at the Evewell in July 2022.
IVF clinics are really in the business of selling hope. When people are spending upwards of £10k on a round of IVF there should be far more rigour and independent analysis of the 'success results' that a clinic claims.
We sort treatment due to having a low AMH. Our NHS  consultant gave us a 3% chance of success.
My advice after having 2 unsuccessful rounds (1st with the ARGC) and 2 miscarriages is to try and relax! Keep yourself healthy and try to be positive. We went on holiday and shortly after coming home we got naturally pregnant and now have a beautiful 9 week old baby!

There is a serious problem with their patient communication. My doctor left mid-treatment and there was no communication to me and this change. I inadvertently find out about the departure while I was sorting out their terrible record …

I chose to have my fertility treatment with ARGC because their published statistics looked incredible. Unfortunately, my experience did not reflect the same standard.

The clinic can be quite difficult to navigate at first, though over time you do find your feet. My first visit was for immune testing — I had even called twice beforehand to confirm the details. However, when I arrived, I was mistakenly treated as if I was there for a monitoring cycle. After some confusion and back-and-forth, I was redirected to another clinic for the correct tests.

When my results came back showing elevated NK cells, I was understandably upset. Dr. Nawal was kind, clear, and supportive, explaining the next steps thoroughly. I later decided to proceed with a TSI (timed intercourse) round with Letrozole.

While the doctors I encountered were excellent — knowledgeable, empathetic, and concise — the communication with the nursing and administrative teams was consistently poor. Messages and emails often went unanswered, sometimes for days, which during treatment can feel like an eternity. I frequently had to repeat information that was already in my file and chase updates that should have been proactively shared.

During my cycle, there were repeated issues with clarity about next steps, medication requirements, and blood testing. On one occasion, I was contacted after leaving the clinic to return and give more blood due to a sample issue. Later, I discovered I had been given insufficient progesterone pessaries for my two-week wait, despite the team knowing I would be abroad. When I reached out for help, the responses were dismissive and lacked accountability.

I eventually submitted an official complaint after returning from treatment abroad. The response I received was defensive, inaccurate in parts, and did not acknowledge the stress caused by their poor communication and administration.

When my treatment ultimately did not work, I still reached out with openness to continue. Sadly, my final interaction — a phone call with the head nurse — was met with hostility and no compassion, despite just having shared the news that my treatment had failed.

The doctors at ARGC are undeniably skilled, and I have full respect for their medical expertise. However, the administrative, nursing, and patient care teams seriously undermine the clinic’s reputation. The lack of communication, empathy, and accountability made what was already an emotionally and financially challenging process far more stressful than it needed to be.

If you are considering ARGC, I would advise going in with caution — their doctors are excellent, but their support systems for patients fall significantly short of the standard you would expect from a leading fertility clinic.

I am sad to leave this review after many years at ARGC. I will start by saying that the admin staff and nurses are amazing. Unfortunately the doctors are so disorganised and frantic that no one knows who you are ever, and this leads to serious problems. ARGC put my health completely at risk (then joked about it) and I would really urge everyone to avoid the clinic for this reason. I lodged a complaint with them but there was no response of course
Awful - there is very poor communication and management of patients' mental health.  It is an enterprise designed to make a profit from women and family's desperation and misery.

ARGC Malpractice Risked My Sister’s Life:

Unfortunately, my sister received treatment at
ARGC for an in vitro fertilization procedure. Despite the protocols indicating that implantation should be avoided if there are signs of ovarian hyper-stimulation, the clinic advised her to proceed with the implantation of two embryos. As a result, she developed ascites, renal failure, and severe electrolyte imbalances, putting her life at risk.

From the onset of the first symptoms, including difficulty breathing, abdominal swelling, nausea, and intolerance to food, my sister visited the clinic multiple times. However, no additional tests were performed, and her condition was downplayed. She was simply advised to increase fluid intake.

Fortunately, my sister went to A&E, where she was attended by the critical care team. They confirmed that her condition was life-threatening. The hospital later confirmed that she was the only patient they had treated with ovarian hyperstimulation syndrome (OHSS) who had embryos implanted. As no reputable clinic would recommend implantation when the ovaries are hyperstimulated.

Currently, she is hospitalized in critical condition due to complications arising from malpractice at ARGC, which failed to apply proper preventive measures and dismissed her initial symptoms, allowing her condition to worsen and putting her life in danger.

Please carefully consider your health and well-being before choosing ARGC for your fertility treatment.

Simply a negative experience through and through. Communication and environment are terrible, egg collection room looks very dodgy and not hygenic, getting information during scans is impossible- they almost can’t be bothered to talk to you, arrogance and entitlement of the doctors starting with Dr Taranissi is unbelievable.. Such high cost for such poor experience and results. It simply is not worth it. Although they have the reputation of ‘delivering results’ I think it is either an old or exaggerated story… Noone was able to provide any data.. Don’t waste your time and money and think that the clinic being on Harley St makes it good.

We had a demanding but overall acceptable experience all the way through egg collection. The problem is after having 10 eggs and 10 embryos fertilized on day 1, there was no check between day 1 and day 5. We were told on day 7 that we had none left. Nobody could explain to us what went wrong - just to try another cycle. Personally, it felt like we were constantly pushed to spend more on tests, and since we didn’t plan to transfer, perhaps they thought we were a bad stat anyway. Time and money wasted. More importantly, it took my husband and I four months to recover from that experience and now need to start over somewhere else.

I'm really very surprised by any of the positive reviews I've read on this site. Our experience at ARGC was so awful. Someone wrote here that it feels like they are selling hope. That's exactly how we found it. The place is hugely overcrowded and so so shabby. We were ushered in from one test to the next with little or no explantation of what it was all for. Each time we went through the door we paid more money and we didn't know why.  After the first consultation we didn't have any connection with the doctor apart from one not very pleasant scan where I asked him what my results meant. He didn't look at me and said that I had a very low chance of getting pregnant and that was it. I was devastated and left the room.

After having spent over £1000 on bloodtests over several weeks we went in to start the treatment. On this day we found out that they needed another £5000 for medication on top of the £5000 we knew about. We hadn't been told about this and when we complained they said we should have asked about how much it was going to cost - which we had done repeatedly. The price list we had been given was very obtuse and hadn't made this remotely clear. I was devastated. We didn't have this money set aside. It felt like our chance of having a baby was taken away from us and we'd thrown away the money we'd spent so far.

Some of the staff were so uncaring. You felt like you were wasting their time with a question. I felt like they just wanted our money. There was no human connection. Yes they get good results for people who are able to pay for it. But that can't be all there is. You should feel cared for and comforted and kept in the light when going through something as potentially as stressful as IVF.

But the good news is we found a clinic in the Czech Republic - in Prague called UNICA. The clinic was clean and calm with a wonderful zen like atmosphere. The doctors available to talk and tell you about your treatment at every step. The and receptionists who know your name and ask how you are. Above all you have a dedicated co-ordinator who answers any questions you have, looks after you, walks in with you before the operation and there for you at the end. She tells you what drugs you are taking and why. She gives you a little pep talk when you need it and reminds you to be realistic when you need that too. In short it was the best care we could have hoped for. For this exceptional treatment, scans and all medication, flights, accommodation, food, concerts in the most beautiful of cities and a holiday of a lifetime the whole thing came to £5000 - about £7000 less than we would have paid at ARGC. We don't yet know whether we are pregnant but the experience was magical and I'd urge anyone thinking about doing IVF to explore options abroad.

The lack of follow up after a traumatic round of treatment which ended up with the hospitalisation of my wife, who had severe OHSS from an egg collection, is disgusting. Complete negligence from the staff at the time - they should have …

ARGC is one of the worst clinics in the U.K. I froze my eggs with them in 2016, got 19 in total, I thought I had preserved my fertility. However in 2020, when I wanted to use my frozen eggs, Dr Sapna continued to insist we should do a fresh cycle, which made no sense to given we I had eggs saved from when I was much younger. Normally When you thaw eggs, survival rate is 90%- ARGC lost 17/19eggs while thawing them.

I have take two opinions since, one from a clinic in the US and one at St Mary’s hospital, both confirmed that their lab must be sub standard and embryologist are not trained properly given our outcome.

We requested a full investigation, which came back with “we followed protocol on the day”. After such a disastrous outcome- Dr Taranassi never spoke with us or emailed us ( even though we requested it). Please do yourself a favour and pick a clinic with modern lab like Care Fertility/CRGH or anyone else really. Absolutely AVOID ARGC- substandard is the only description.

Paid them a deposit last year for an appointment that never happened due to lack of information (not) communicated by staff at ARGC. Was told I'd get a refund but am still waiting on the money. Very disappointing.

Months ago we started treatment here. Told us it will cost around £6000-£8000. At the end we paid £15,000 for nothing. Had a Miscarriage.  Some of the staff really kind. Few of them are so rude. Doctors are very kind and helpful. Everything …

I abhorred my experience there:
- Paper printed medical records
- Dated decor, blue carpet and dusty fake flowers
- Conflicting information between the scanning nurse and the nurses
- Super rushed atmosphere at the time when you as a patient are the most vulnerable
- Zero to none consultant time
- Zero to non consideration for the patient time and wallet
- They will insist you have milk while you are in stimulation phase, I am vegetarian and follow a lactose free diet. There is no scientific ground for recommending this.

Would not go back there even if I was gifted another chance...

Terrible experience! Trust me when I say, do not use this company. Communication is terrible, service is terrible, constantly being told different things by different people. At so many occasions we were let down. We are currently in the process of communicating with the ombudsman. Please beware

Very rushed, stressful, impersonal and too expensive.  After spending 12k I did not even have any eggs left over to freeze!!.  -ve result. When i approached the team seeking counselling as i was upset all they did was try to sell me further sessions!!!!! I left deeply upset. V bad experience.
I had very bad experience with them. One if the consultants didn’t write the consultation outcome correctly which resulted in incorrect timing of meds. Unfortunately this place is not for me
Its a scam! I booked for an appointment 3 workdays ago, no one answers the phone. No calls received

Disappointing.

The wait times and bedside manner are just as disappointing as the NHS and when I am spending hundreds of pounds for a service I expect better.  Most disappointingly, the doctor did not introduce herself nor say hello, when I walked in she just ordered me to undress and get on the chair. I get that the practice busy but empathy and conversational skills is apart of the job and not using them is not how you make someone feel comfortable before an internal pelvic scan.

I have also been emailing for a number of months requesting a copy of my ultrasound report to which I have had no response. I am hoping this review will escalate a response from your company.

I am still waiting for my treatment and might change my feedback later, but I have to tell that if a donor is required, you need to deal with “the lab”, which seems like a separate set up with little communication with nurses and doctors. They answer emails one a week, don’t communicate well about what’s required, and possible delaying my treatment with no concern whatsoever. Any enquiry is answered again one week later. Any respectable clinic can’t have a lab operating like this!

Terrible experience, despite high cost the clinic is extremely disorganised (my file couldn’t be found several times they had booked me in), terrible communication (nurses on the same day providing different information), lack of clarity on the full process and costs. There were consistent delays and you feel rushed at every step. It is managed like a production line. The experience has definitely not been positive and if I went back in time I would not choose ARGC

The main issue is with how they treat people. You are just a number. You wait 30 min to 1 hour for a scan, they don’t try to speed things up or optimise, they don’t care. You never get privacy when speaking about your situation, it’s always in front of 5 other people waiting by the front desk. The morning of my egg collections, one of the nurses yelled at me because 1 page in my file was not filled in. The first day I collected the meds, no one had told me I would get a large bag full of meds which I had to take with me to a conference in Canary Wharf.
March 2020 update: the IVF with ARGC did not work, the process was hard physically and mentally. I then fell pregnant naturally 1 year later and now have a healthy and happy baby! So hopefully there is light at the end of the tunnel for those of you researching IVF.

We had an unsuccessful round of IVF at the ARGC in Jan 2022.
IVF clinics are really in the business of selling hope. When people are spending upwards of £10k on a round of IVF there should be far more rigour and independent analysis of the 'success results' that a clinic claims.
We sort treatment due to having a low AMH. Our NHS consultant gave us a 3% chance of success.
My advice after having 2 unsuccessful rounds (2nd with the Evewell) and 2 miscarriages is to try and relax! Keep yourself healthy and try to be positive. We went on holiday and shortly after coming home we got naturally pregnant and now have a beautiful 9 week old baby!

Trying to build a family through fertility treatment is already exhausting and heartbreaking. What we didn’t expect was to also have to manage a clinic.

Our experience at Create Fertility (St Paul’s, London) has been marked by poor communication and a lack of coordination. The clinic’s “patient coordinator” model hasn’t functioned as a support system for us—there have been long stretches with no proactive contact, no clear ownership of next steps, and uncertainty about who is responsible for our case. Instead of feeling guided, we’ve often felt we were chasing information and stitching the process together ourselves.

Appointments have also felt poorly managed, with delays that add even more strain to days that are already emotionally loaded. When you need answers quickly, getting hold of the clinic can be difficult, and responses are not consistently timely.

If you are pursuing the egg donor route that involves working with IVI Madrid, be aware that coordination between clinics may fall heavily on you as the patient. The contrast has been noticeable: the Madrid-side transfer process has been efficient, and the blood draw staff in London have been consistently good. Unfortunately, the overall London-side support has not matched what this kind of journey demands.

This review is to help future patients know what to expect and to encourage Create to improve the basic standards of communication, continuity, and care.

UPDATE: I emailed a complaint in about the lack of care I'm receiving with hopes that it would help progress our treatment. Instead, I'm told that the the complaint needs to be resolved BEFORE my treatment can resume!! So my treatment hinges on Create fixing poor internal processes that have been on going for years now.  I feel that I am being penalized as a patient for raising a concern.  Despite raising a complaint, I have yet to hear from anyone for days now.  Time is ticking away and we're not closer to having the family we dreamed of.

I would give negative 100 if I could, back in 2024, I was 36 year-old (turining 37) when I under went a full IVF cycle with ABC IVF/Create Fertility. Their one2one conselling session costed me almost £200 per hour and I was not given much information nor support, their service was rushed, and the week when I had my egg retrival surgery, their building was out of power for 3 days. Ended up failed with my non-refundable cycle, they blamed me for being too old and could unlikely never have my own biological child ever. I felt publicly humiliated when the embryologist told me all of these, and so I had formally complained this clinic to HFEA. Then, I had a mental breakdown and suffered 1 month of hospitalisation and further 6 months of recovery. During this time, I had my current boyfriend who encouraged me to try conceive naturally with him. I got pregnant after first try, now I have our lovely daughter in my arms. This clinic is a highly commercialised and they can falsely make you feel good about yourself or have wrong perception about your fertility, so you put your money down to their services. However, their embryologists were incredibly unprofessional and nasty when things aren't going smoothly. BE VERY CAREFUL with choosing your clinic and services if you want to go through full IVFs.

I have been a patient at Create Fertility since September 2024, and I am now in the process of moving my embryos to another clinic. I feel it’s important to share my experience so that others are fully informed of the poor communication and …

An “experienced nurse” did the blood test and used her fingers to wipe my blood instead of the cloth and tape, resulting in the bruise lasting for more than 10 days. Seriously concern around hygiene and professionalism.

If you want to experience the same level of service, feel free to sign on the 3-cycle contract and get you £20k locked in. No refund after 6 weeks of cancellation which blocked me from exploring other clinics in such a time sensitive situation.

Very poor service with hidden charges that they don't mention on their website such as having to pay to travel to Spain and PAYG medications for donors. I left there wondering if they are scammers so will be contacting the authorities.

It took me a year to recover from the way I was treated in this clinic because of my age and the results.
I had a good promising first consultation.
However,I am aware and informed that the fertility tests good results can be good but does not mean 100% success.i had 10 eggs retrieved and 7 of good grades with 6 of best grades. I Was updated the first 3 days of embryos then afterward received an email that disappeared before I read it on a weekend. Contact the clinic to find out what it was about and was told I will be call the next day or 2days. Anxious to know how my 6 good embryos I was informed of on Thursday and Friday were doing.
When I call in afternoon, I was told that no I am will received the call on Tuesday instead. Call again on Tuesday, I was told I will received a call on Wednesday. On Wednesday I received a call from an ambriologist I never spoken before telling me what do you want us to do with your
one embryo that is left, conduct the biopsy test done on it.
Not even explained to me what happened to the other embryos which were according to the information received on Friday were all growing well with good amount of cells as expected.
When I asked the question, the person.without any consideration of my feelings just said, they dud not survive the biopsy due to your age.
Since then,I did not speak again to either a consultant and the embryologist I have been spoken to before.
The person was so ear close and no emphaty or care  at all when speaking to me about it.
I know I made payment for all ivf procedure from start to the end. I did not have any implantation for which I was asked to pay. They made me me pay for all drugs even progesterone that was supposed to be used when I already pregnant before hands.
I was denied any kind of re-emboursement  for all procedure I did not have.
I paid even for embryos conservation for a year which did not even happened.
I think my care after my embryos got probably accidentally destroyed by someone at that clinic without informing me and missing the dates of my update on the progress was really really unfair.
To have the report of anything else to forward to my GP became another big deal on emails back and forward were I was turned around because they were asking me pay for the report after destroying my embryos.I said that because before the destruction of my embryos I was given every scan and consultant report without even asking for it.
I did find it hard to believed.  That one private care should that worst compared to the public care only because they know that care is not accessible to everyone in the public sector.

I find it hard to believe that there are reviews for this clinic which are more than 1 or 2 stars. They took weeks of messing us about with requests for various blood tests which we already did and these were sent to them. Communication is …

I was consulted at this clinic five months ago, checked my AMH and scan.In just a few days my results were very different from those from the last clinic. I think this is very abnormal, At my urging, they offered me a free test a month …

Would strongly recommend anyone considering going with Create London to avoid. I can't emphasise enough how poor the communication and service was. I had a positive pregnancy test result but the nurse I spoke to didn't bother to tell me …

The level of patient care is absolutely appalling.  I’m shocked that a clinic is allowed to treat patients this way.  It’s impossible to get through on the phone and when you do you get through to a receptionist who tells you your patient …

I started my IVF journey with Create during Covid and at first, they seemed to be very informative and have time for my questions. But after the egg retrieval was done, I couldn’t get hold of my patient coordinator. …

The time of our Zoom consultation was changed by email at 4pm the day before without actually checking if it was convenient for us. We changed our plans to accommodate it and then the Consultant never even came!!!

Awful, never again, if you use the clinic in Manchester good luck, the consultant turned up late to both consultations, no one even bothered to contact us of delays, and once things were moving forward we were promised to timelines that …

Very frustrating as we were treated as customers not patients a lot of hidden fee and a lot of medication sent before starting the treatment while there wasn’t even need to use as they told me after initial scans the medication is not …

Avoid avoid avoid! Since going to another clinic it's made me realise just how bad Create were. Felt very much like just another number to them. Different doctors all the time, always running late. phone lines constantly busy. Very little support and understanding given. Miss-leading prices. IVF and infertility is hard enough, do yourself a favour and pick a different clinic!

Had some blood tests here recently. The actual test was not a pleasant experience. They have a corridor queuing system which was a bit like a factory. It wasn't explained to me so a couple of people got ahead of me. It felt so impersonal and took a lot longer than I expected as I was originally given an appointment time. I would strongly urge anyone considering CREATE Fertility to think very carefully before proceeding.

Our experience has been characterised by poor communication, a complete lack of coordination between departments, and a level of patient care that has steadily deteriorated over time.

We were led to believe we would be proceeding with a frozen embryo transfer this cycle, took time off work for appointments, and only at the last minute were told that basic prerequisite steps had not even been arranged. These were things that should have been identified and coordinated well in advance. The result was an entirely avoidable delay to treatment.

What has been most shocking is the complete inability to get hold of our assigned patient coordinator. Despite repeated emails, phone calls, formal complaints, and requests for escalation, weeks have passed with virtually no meaningful communication. Multiple staff members acknowledged internally that responses were overdue and that management had been “chased”, yet still nobody contacted us.

The repeated explanation given to us has been that the clinic is “exceptionally busy”. Frankly, if that is the case then the clinic has clearly taken on more patients than it can adequately support and manage appropriately. As private patients undergoing fertility treatment, we do not believe it is unreasonable to expect basic communication, coordination, and continuity of care.

Every department appears to operate in isolation, with responsibility constantly shifting elsewhere while no one takes ownership. The role of a patient coordinator should surely be to bridge those gaps, yet in our experience that coordination has been entirely absent.

Sadly, this sits within wider concerns we have had regarding clinical decision-making and informed consent. We were advised to freeze one embryo at day 3, while the remaining embryos continued in culture but ultimately did not progress to blastocyst stage. This frozen embryo is now our one and only embryo.

Looking back, we do not feel we were provided with sufficiently balanced or patient-specific information to make a truly informed decision regarding freezing at day 3 versus continuing culture to day 5. We were later advised there is no clear evidence base demonstrating improved outcomes with this approach, yet the implications of the decision — emotionally, financially, and practically — were never properly explained to us.

Most concerningly, during a follow-up appointment after egg collection, the consultant openly acknowledged that the clinic receives “lots of complaints” regarding this practice and stated they “might be changing” it. Hearing that after the fact only reinforced our feeling that concerns around this approach were already well known internally, yet we were not properly informed beforehand.

As non-medical patients, you place enormous trust in a fertility clinic to guide you through hugely emotional and complex decisions. Instead, we have been left dealing with months of uncertainty surrounding the viability of our only embryo, while continuing to pay storage fees and repeatedly chase for basic communication.

At times, the clinic has felt less like a healthcare provider focused on patient care and more like a profit-driven operation, where communication and support rapidly decline once patients are within the system. The repeated delays, lack of accountability, ongoing storage fees, and constant need for us to chase updates have only reinforced that feeling.

At this stage, we feel completely powerless. CREATE currently has our one and only embryo in storage, while we struggle simply to get responses or move treatment forward. We are now actively exploring transferring our embryo to another clinic because we no longer have confidence in the level of care, communication, or organisation being provided.

Fertility treatment is emotionally exhausting enough without feeling ignored, unsupported, and passed between departments. We would not recommend this clinic.

I'd like to express that I feel disappointed finding out about the procedure Create takes when deciding/offering to freeze an embryo on day 3. When we received the call to ask as if we would like to freeze that embryo, we were told that it was a good quality. Later one, we found out that this embryo would still have to go to a previous and additional process of thawing before being transfer in which there is a possibility it could be lost, i.e. it cannot have a good quality to be transfer. During one of the last calls, I had with a nurse, I was told that this is normal procedure of Create. To decide to freeze embryos on day 3. I was also told that other clinics do not do this anymore, while Create still does. I felt disappointed when given this information as this can be easily interpreted as being given higher hopes. This procedure, I was told, is done to prevent or to make sure there is one embryo frozen. However, this is not 100% accurate as at that moment, we as a couple, or other couples, do not know that there is an additional step required for the embryo to be transferred. In other words, I feel like I have two embryos frozen (chances for a potential pregnancy) and one maybe. As you can imagine, this feels disheartening considering the high financial investment and effort put into this procedure.  Furthermore, the day 3 embryo, after three days of cultivation, it did not survive and was discarded. The quality was not good enough to freeze again. We keep asking ourselves why wasn't it cultivated from the beginning further? We have ask Create and we still get no answer. We've been told that this was in perfect quality to freeze and it was done just in case none of the other would survive. This creates uncertainty. I interpret is as a way to say add an extra 'successful' frozen embryo as a result of the treatment when in reality it isn't! Therefore, I highly suggest to couples to let their embryos develop until day 5 or until they are in great quality for direct transfer.

I received treatment with Create St Paul’s in June of 2025 and since moving clinics wanted to leave a review of my experience. I’ll start by saying that all of the healthcare professionals I encountered there were very kind, considerate and extremely supportive in helping me through my first experience of IVF.

After a lot of research we decided to go with Create. A huge deciding factor in this was its location with it having a clinic in St Albans as well as central London. I have a fairly hectic work schedule so this was important to minimise stress around apts. Unfortunately throughout the process we only had two apt. in St Albans one of which was cancelled so I would not take this location as much to do with the clinic.

The difficulty we had with create was around the clinic being very unorganised and lacking in personalised care. You see a different Dr at every appointment leading to a lack of consistency. The biggest issue for us was the patient coordinators. The idea is you are assigned someone who supports you throughout the process who helps with all of the admin side.

Our patient coordinator changed 4 times and were completely unorganised to the point where I often had to follow up and chase up admin and apts. This added a lot more stress into the process, managing everything alongside work and I felt I couldn’t trust them to be on top of everything.

Following our unsuccessful results the doctors did a review and said this was a case of ‘luck’ and they recommended we go again with the same protocol. Knowing what I know now, I am absolutely baffled with this response as there were a whole host of easy diagnostic tests we could have done and things we could have tweaked moving forward. We have since found a different clinic where we had drastically different positive results.

Looking back at the experience with create, I feel they are a very run of the mill IVF clinic that won’t be able to go above a very basic IVF protocol. They offer treatment for NHS and private patients however, I feel we very much got an NHS experience but at the price of a private clinic.

I initially contacted The Fertility & Gynaecology Academy due to a misunderstanding with the contact information I found online.
