The doctor discussed Intrauterine Insemination, emphasising its low success rate, and quoted the treatment at £1,500 to £2,000, which is considerably higher than the £1,100 listed on their website. Similarly, for IVF treatment, he quoted £6,000, significantly above the website's prices of £3,800 for IVF and £4,750 for an IVF-One Cycle Package. This pattern of quoting higher prices than those listed led us to suspect an intentional strategy to increase patient expenditure, undermining our trust in the clinic's transparency and honesty.

Furthermore, when my husband requested a written report of our assessment, Dr. Gorgy informed us that the clinic only provides verbal feedback, denying us any tangible documentation of our consultation. This approach felt dismissive and left us without essential reference material.

My husband was advised to get hormone tests, and the finance team advised us to use their in-house lab for these tests. Before finalizing any decisions, we carefully reviewed the costs involved. In the end, we were confronted with a billing summary of £760, comprising £280 for the consultation and an unexpectedly high £480 for the blood tests. This was in stark contrast to the anticipated costs, especially since the basic hormone blood tests were listed at £70 on their website. Yet, we were quoted £240 under the "Hormone Profile" category, with an additional two tests at £120 each. Given these discrepancies and the lack of clear justification for the extra charges, we decided not to proceed with the blood tests, sensing something fishy in their billing practices. This decision was driven by the dramatic difference in expected versus actual billing, which raised significant concerns about the clinic's transparency and integrity. It appeared as though there was a deliberate attempt to manipulate billing categories for financial gain, indicating a profoundly troubling approach to financial practices.

Our interaction with the admin staff and the accounting team, especially concerning the lab tests, further compounded our decision not to pursue treatment at this clinic. The perceived enthusiasm of the admin staff felt insincere upon leaving, and the pressuring tactics of the accounting team regarding the lab tests were particularly off-putting.

In conclusion, our visit to The Fertility & Gynaecology Academy was disappointing. We encountered a lack of empathy, flexibility, and a respect for our autonomy and time. The inconsistent pricing and the refusal to provide written assessments, coupled with a concerning billing experience, diminished our trust in the clinic. We felt viewed not as individuals in need of support but as potential sources of increased revenue.

Based on our experience, we cannot recommend The Fertility & Gynaecology Academy. We will seek assistance elsewhere, where our health and well-being are prioritised, and transparency is a given.

Me and my partner had a video consultation with the doctor. (She had several treatments done at the clinic before, so the doctor  knows her medical history.) Our agenda of the consultation was to discuss results of tests that were recommended by the doctor. Not to mention that each consolation with the doctor (each time you need to address any question to the doctor) is extremely expensive 170+ pounds. When such a high premium is paid you expect to get a corresponding service. 50% of scheduled 30 min zoom talk both audio and video just froze - we could not hear anything. During the rest of the time we were able to formulate a few questions. Almost none of the questions were answered. Definitely nothing specific, no reasoning at all. When a person claims to be a specialist in a certain field,  immunology in this case, he/she should be able to interpret both clinical history of a patient and results of tests and and at least try to connects the dots - to provide some sort of reasoning, which makes him believe that a certain medication is of a benefit. I didn't get a feeling that a person I was talking to had good knowledge  of immunology. It was rather: try this medication because (we can provide it) and in general it works better than other medications - very business oriented. One of test was LAD - and after some hours of researching and reading replies (that are publicity available) from other doctors to patients with similar test results and clinical history, I'm almost 100% that the doctor didn't even highlight result that were of relevance, but instead pointed to a completely different direction. Luckily for people working in this field there are not so many medications to chose from , and therefore even people with a limited knowledge of the domain can "successfully" operate in this field.

I had a very unfortunate experience of coming across this clinic and its doctors. After i came to terms with the lack of the actual result ; My later regret was combined with both anger for the waste of money but mostly for being given a false hope by what can only be described  a totally unsympathetic and robotic doctor (along with his mismanagement and misadvise on my situation). 3 unsuccessful IVF cycles and money down the drain (where I was charged for the 3rd cycle  that involved nothing but one scan and one "supposed egg extraction" ) I later learnt that they could have just advised against the last IVF cycle completely in order not to waste my money and not to give me the false hope..... We couldn't help it but my husband and I both felt somewhat the "extortion approach" but alas also knew that we were partially to blame for  trusting ourselves into the incompetence of these doctors. My advise to other women out there, do your research promptly and thoroughly before committing to your IFV team....I will add though that some of the less senior members of staff at the clinic were pleasant and understanding....so justly.. i wont say much negative about them...

Really disappointing & distressing experience with Dr. Gorgys clinic- both the service & staff, from start to finish. I travelled from another country to attend an appointment last August 2022. They missed some of the bloods on the day - which can happen but then zero help or support to sort the issue - I was left to arrange the missed bloods in my home country with a third party that they deal with - it was very stressful all round. Spent months trying to get information from FGA around what bloods were carried out on me. 7 months later it is still going on. Am waiting on a partial refund & again zero help. I could write pages on what happened. Anyway, in a nutshell, genuinely shocked at how they carry out (or dont) their business. I would not recommend this clinic on any level.

Update 12/05/2023 in response to Dr. Gorgys Clinic Reply

This response is interesting, given what happened, hence the update - the issue happened in August 2022 and I received ZERO help or support from from Dr. Gorgys Clinc to resolve the problem at that time - as stated in my original review - I was left to sort bloods in my home country & set everything up with no support from Dr. Gorgys clinic for the whole entire process.

This, despite the fact that, again, as referenced in my original review, we traveled to the UK and paid for all services with Dr. Gorgys clinic and just because you outsource part of your services, I, as YOUR customer, was left to deal with this outsourced company directly. Shocking & disappointing.

In addition, your reference to “have been communicating with you ever since to resolve the issue” - you did not communicate to resolve the above issue where we were left for weeks trying to sort the mess & issue with bloods. What you did communicate on was the minor issue of a partial refund, at a much later stage, when we had been left to sort the actual problems on our own.
In addition, the apology you reference as made was - an apology on behalf of the 3rd party - I have no record of where Dr. Gorgys clinic took responsibility or apologised for anything themselves.

BE CAREFUL! I donated my eggs in this Clinic over a year ago.
They are very unprofessional and rude.
I ended up in A&E with OHSS because of them and my life was at risk.
I'm in process of reporting this clinic and especially Dr. G who has over stimulated my eggs and tried to get away with it when I started to feel unwell and ended up in the hospital.
And okay maybe they helped women to get pregnant and have their dream baby but the way they treat us - donors is shocking and had a big impact on my physical and mental health. I will never forgive you.

I have had two treatments of IUI with Dr. Gorgy after someone I knew had a number of treatments with him. Unfortunately, we don't exactly share the same experience and I can relate to the other reviews that the whole experience is very money driven.

Dr. Gorgy takes very little into account your past history and hardly writes down any notes or remembers anything from previous appointments. This is something i was prepped for by my friend who attended the clinic, so I knew to write a diary for each appointment.

I had one cycle of IUI with Gorgy which all went well and all to plan, however no pregnancy at no fault of the clinic. Sadly the second cycle did not go as well, where he completely missed my ovulation by a couple days. Somehow I was convinced to proceed with my IUI, and out of desperation I agreed. I felt mislead as the chances are actually known to be very small when performing IUI a couple days after ovulation which was not mentioned on the day.

During this cycle I ended up hyperstimulated and suspected for an ectopic pregnancy for an event that happened prior to this IUI. I notified the nurses that I was going away around this period and no advice was given not to travel when I was due to fly 7 hours overseas. Had my pregnancy turned out to be ectopic I might have died mid flight.

Unfortunately, while abroad I ended up in hospital due illness from being hyperstimuled and also underwent investigations for an ectopic pregenancy which turned out to be negative. From the experience I found it really amazing how the medical clinic treating me did not advise me not to travel when I mentioned it to them, particularly when it comes to potentially life threatening issues.

Another review mentions the Dr. being late, I can vouch for this as on three occasions I arrived for an ultrasound over my two treatments with Dr. Gorgy not being there that day when promised and then and just passively sent for a blood test alone.

Personally, I found the majority of the staff apart from Jeana a bit ignorant. On a number of occasions I had texted the nurses during my miscarriage and they did not reply back to my texts.

Lastly, a particularly uncomfortable situation being sent into a basement for payment each session where the staff don't have the courtesy to even look you in the eye. I felt like that handing over a couple thousand was a nuisance to them.

Since I've had treatment with the NHS and I can hands down say that the treatment is ten folds better. Sadly, I won't be recommending this clinic as I felt there was serious lack of care.

Extremely poor service. First I called and ask if I can book an appointment for test and I was told “yes” and the receptionist book me for the test and even made me pay a deposit. After one hour she called me back saying she  did a mistake and first I need to have a consultation and then the test... but instead of giving me an option to have my money back since she took deposit for a service they don't offer, she told me that I can still go for the test first and have a consultation later on... which it doesn't make sense but anyway I was desperate to do the test so I agreed. So I went there at 12h(my appointment ) and waited there almost 40min to find out that I will have a consultation instead of a test. I’ve paid £570 for the test and consultation and I was told that the results will be ready in 3-4weeks. 5 weeks later, no one contacted me so I decided to give them a call, they told me to send an email, I’ve sent one and I am still waiting for response.....one week.... One week for a response... response which simply could be “we are sorry but there is a delay with your test” But... NO, they are not even bothered to get back to me. So much money for such a disrespectful service, I will not continue with the rest of the tests I was offered by them as they are not reliable!

I cannot unfortunately recommend this place. Dr Gorgy charged us £230 for a consultation, which resulted only in him providing the complete pricelist for his services, none of our history was analysed, and no specific or useful recommendations were made - instead just a recommendation that we try a bit of everything and we can decide on what to prioritise. This "consultation" could have lasted less than fifteen minutes if he'd had his way. Nothing was explained, just the technical names of tests were listed off. I insisted on asking for explanations, which were still unclear and ultimately not useful. I'd recommend avoiding this place.
Pleaae do not respond to my low rating stating im not a patient. My wife and I are both registered. …

Very money orientated and doctor constantly late.
Doctor not showing up at time of egg collection with 2 patients in front of me... Never had a scan before going into theatre to check if follicles were still there. Was told my eggs did not fertilize. This is the only clinic where my eggs did not fertilize...  and it happened twice.

I would not go back - shallow approach .. fees that exceed any insurance premium for no reason .. silly chats
To top up the second consultation is over the phone max on 2 min charged at the rate of £250
Give us a break

I have waited a while to write this review for various reasons. However, given the potential negative impact on many other women who might have the same misfortune of coming across Dr Sarah Glynne, I thought I must follow up with a review.

I went to see Sarah Glynne following some hormonal issues post covid. During the appointment, I was told that I was in "early perimenopause", despite having impeccable blood work and no other evidence outside of a range of symptoms (some of which overlap with perimenopause, but can also be seen in Long covid). She then suggested I would need to be on HRT until age 51 (I am 30). She told me that she would keep upping my dose up to a maximum, until I find relief from my symptoms. I was also told that lifestyle adjustments cannot balance hormones and have no impact. She told me that covid damages the ovaries and that they only "bounce back in younger women".

Naturally, I left the appointment very demoralised to say the least.

I then consulted a highly regarded and, unlike Sarah Glynne, actually qualified Gynaecologist, to get a second opinion. Long story short and to paraphrase, he said that her recommendations and assessment were NONSENSE. There is no evidence that I am in perimenopause and also no evidence of Long covid having lasting impact on ovaries. He also told me that my symptoms would be different in frequency and cadence if it was indeed perimenopause. And that my symptoms were more likely a result of dysregulation post covid, which can also impact hormonal balance (but this is NOT perimenopause). He prescribed me a different treatment option which was much more sensible, and also recommended lifestyle changes as he believes these DO make a big difference. And indeed they have done. Sarah Glynne was entirely wrong in her assessment and her recommendations were dangerous.

I am writing this because:

- A. I want my money back for the £300 consultation which was not only a waste of money but also caused more harm than good (especially to my mental health). I am also grateful I followed my gut and did NOT go on HRT given the risks.

- B. I do not want other poor young women to come across Sarah Glynne and have the misfortune to be told they are perimenopausal when this is NOT the case.

To conclude, I believe women struggling with post covid issues with hormones consult a qualified Gynaecologist before making such conclusions about their health, and taking actions that can cause serious harm.

Sarah Glynne has clearly ignored the Hippocratic oath of "first do no harm", as harm she did cause. I do hope Claire Mellon & Associates offer reasonable compensation following this message, as I do not want to escalate things further.

I’m extremely disappointed with the level of customer service I’ve received from 25 Harley Street Gynaecology Clinic. When you’re paying Harley Street prices, you expect a standard of service that reflects that – unfortunately, that’s far from what I experienced.

While Dr. Khan was lovely and professional, the same cannot be said for the administrative side of the clinic. The secretary’s communication is poor, and I’ve felt ignored and frustrated throughout the process. One of my blood tests had to be re-done due to what appears to be misplacement or contamination, and although two of the tests were processed, one wasn’t – leading to an unnecessary return trip.

Even worse, I had a follow-up appointment scheduled, and I received no call from the doctor. I emailed – no reply. I called – I was told my results weren’t ready and I’d simply have to wait longer. At this point, it has been more than 10 days, and I have no clarity, no results, and no follow-up. All this after spending £900 on a consultation and tests.

This is not what you expect from a Harley Street clinic – communication has been terrible, and the lack of responsiveness is completely unacceptable. I’m genuinely upset with how I’ve been treated and would caution others to carefully consider whether this clinic can truly offer the level of care and professionalism their pricing suggests.

Really bad experience at this clinic. I was double charged for my blood tests at the fault of a new member of staff and despite chasing for several weeks, have still not received my refund and have been fobbed off by several different members of staff

In addition, my overpriced follow up phone call was very inadequate. Dr Victoire called late, had lost my notes and blood results, and delivered some serious and upsetting news with very little explanation. She failed to give me any advice on how to administer my prescribed medication. It took an additional week for her to send through the blood tests and scan reports, after a lot of chasing.

I came to this clinic because of its good reviews and have been sorely let down, whilst at the same time trying to grapple with the medical issues that I am experiencing.

After reading some great reviews I’m sorry to say that I had a rather poor experience with Dr Khan and Harley Street Gynaecology.

I felt that in my first consultation he was ok, quite blunt, but ok. It became quite clear that in the second consultation he hadn’t all of read my test results and was dropping in quite big diagnosis as he was reading my results whilst on the call. I had to ask “what about my blood tests?” It was all very rushed and disorganised. I also really didn’t appreciate the tone in which he was speaking to me, which is not at all that of a caring doctor dealing with sensitive issues. Having spoken to a number of my female friends, they have been taken aback by some of the things I was told.

There were a number of mix ups with my appointment times and I had 2 calls outside of agreed times. My appointment was brought forward because of an emergency (which I would have thought an emergency would result in pushing an appointment back?) that’s fine as these things happen, but this wasn’t communicated to me at all so i missed one of my calls and then became nervous when my second call was missed and i had been waiting for 45 minutes. My next call happened outside of the new time that was agreed to.

I was sent a blood test for cancer over email without this being explained to me first. That clearly caused me concern and was really the last straw.

Finally the doctor’s letter was extremely blunt and not at all what I’ve come to expect based on my experience with other clinicians. It caused me more worry than anything.

I decided to get a second opinion and I couldn’t be happier. In the space of 2 days, I’ve had my surgery, extensive tests and I have a great plan in place with a caring doctor who has put to bed some of the sweeping diagnosis that Dr Khan gave me.

The experience I had at Harley Street Gynaecology was horrendous. I booked with Mr Khan a) because of the reviews the clinic has and b) because when booking into a clinic on Harley Street, one would expect the best care. What I can't understand is how the reviews describe Mr Khan as unhurried, friendly, and truly caring. I had the complete opposite experience.

Firstly, and most importantly, Mr Khan gave me an incorrect prescription. The dosage was unclear and it would have been extremely dangerous for me to take what was written on the prescription. When I spoke to Nicky, she and Mr Khan denied that they had given me the incorrect prescription and took no responsibility, instead simply blaming the pharmacy. Nicky asked me why I didn't just use the online pharmarcy. I was not told about the online pharmacy (because the appointment was so hurried?). In fact, I specifically asked Mr Khan where I should collect the prescription and he said my local pharmacy. Luckily, I double checked the dosage I was being prescribed online but this was not at all clear. After paying £140 for the incorrect prescription, I was grateful that Mr Khan offered me a £100 reimbursement but in the same phonecall, Nicky then asked me to leave a review, stating that Mr Khan had (I quote) "bent over backwards" to help me. This NOT the case, as it was Mr Khan's fault that I received the wrong prescription in the first place.

It says on the website that the initial consultation should last around 30 minutes. Well, I was in the waiting room for 45 minutes, but only 5-10 minutes for the consultation, in which time my file was read (surely this should have been read before). I didn't meet the friendly man that Mr Kahn is described to be. He barely looked at me, asked me a few questions to which he had no reply to, and didn't smile once. I actually felt quite uncomfortable sat in his office.

I have paid almost £1000 for 10 minutes with Mr Khan, a blood test and a cervical screening, as well as medication that has not worked for me - it has in fact done the absolute opposite of what I was hoping for. I understand that there isn't a clear fix and you have to try different things sometimes, but it feels like there is absolutely no care and it is instead all about the money. Surely there should be some check in or follow up to see how I'm doing/reacting to the medication - especially for the price paid. What Mr Khan prescribed me has had a horrible effect on me and I'm in a really bad way. You have to pay a further £200 for a follow up consultation, otherwise you'll never have any check-in to see how it's all going. I'm very happy to pay this for a clinic that is actually good, but not this one. I emailed Nicky to see whether I was likely going to have to have more tests in a follow up consultation (meaning more money, so I was just checking) and was instead phoned to book in, as Nicky said she's "not medical" so I was asked to pay £200 upfront and book.

Nicky comes across as a very nice person throughout the process, until you say anything slightly negative about your experience/Mr Khan, at which point she turns very unprofessional and blames your bad experience on you. To quote from one of her emails to me when I emailed to complain (please note that I did not ask for any money back or "blackmail" them as Nicky accused me of. "What is it you want from him? He already has given you money off which I told him for…. Sorry I can not abide someone speaking so awfully about him when it is not true. So as for the previous email please go ahead Miss Jones, no thank you and free to do what ever you want but blackmail – Absolutely NOT. So disgusting and shocked Miss Jones."

The only thing I got out of the appointment was finding out I have a condition that the cervical screening picked up but I was told nothing about the condition or advised how to deal with it at all. There are much better clinics out there so do not waste your time or energy on this one.

EDIT: I didn't leave positive feedback prior to this and was not offered a reduced consultation fee. Lies.

I’ve been dealing with a chronic issue for a long time, and after exhausting my options through the NHS, I decided to invest in private healthcare because I wanted this problem gone. Choosing Harley Street was a major financial decision for me, especially as it is outside of my budget range, but I believed it would provide a higher standard of care and service that would help me.
Unfortunately, the experience has been far below my expectations.
The clinic initially provided the pharmacist with the wrong prescription details. During this time, I contacted the clinic several times with basic questions because I was confused about my prescriptions, yet responses were slow and sometimes took days. Eventually, the pharmacist informed me that the clinic had sent the wrong prescription information.
I was then told that a corrected prescription would be issued. However, it has now been over a week of me chasing the clinic with no resolution, no meaningful updates, and very poor communication throughout. The only emails I have consistently received were requests to pay for the prescription again in order for it to be sent to me, despite the issue originating from the clinic’s mistake. Realistically, this should have been resolved immediately and the corrected prescription sent without expecting me to pay again.
What makes this even more frustrating is that before payment was made, I received repeated duplicate emails chasing invoices for both the service and medication. Yet when I needed support, advice, or clarification, responses became slow, blunt, and lacking empathy.
The overall tone of the service has felt cold and impersonal. I am not from a wealthy background — I came here hoping this treatment would genuinely improve my quality of life, and I have spent a significant amount of money, I’ve even travelled from one city to another to make that happen. Because of that, this experience has been incredibly disappointing.

We attended The Lister in November 2023 for IVF embryo treatment.

The Lister excels in providing a conveyor belt approach that often leaves you waiting 1-2 hours for each appointment. The final week leading up to egg collection I was required to come in for blood tests and ultrasound monitoring every day, fair enough. But each time meant 2-3, sometimes even 4 hours of my day gone.

The in-patient experience and process after extraction was poorly monitored, with an extremely painful recovery. They gave me a very bungled catheter treatment to help me pass water after egg collection, and the following day I developed a high fever and infection, evidently I was going through OHSS. Honestly I’ve had far better and more competent general in-patient hospital treatments by the NHS than here.

In the end they extracted 30 eggs, of the 30 only 16 were usable for ICSI. Of those only 4 became embryos, and after genetic testing only 2 were good, one of which was just a B grade embryo. Pretty unimpressive results to go from 30 follicles stimulated and 30 eggs collected to just 2 embryos, only one of which was of an average A quality. They expressed surprise themselves and then quickly dismissed it as being down to my egg quality and or my partner’s sperm health.

I don’t believe the quality of the result was our fault. Other clinics help you prepare your body in advance and tailor the hormones far better for the best possible chance of having good egg quality retrieval. This place was just a conveyor belt and treated you mostly like everyone else.

Whilst we have 2 embryos, it was a painful and impersonal experience with very unimpressive results.

Expensive, potentially dangerous and not worth it. There are better clinics out there - avoid this one and do your research.

We were very disappointed. The doctor was hard to communicate with and seemed unconcerned. Staff were responsive with appointments, but overall the clinic felt more like a business machine than a patient-focused service. We have since moved and feel much better supported elsewhere.

I'm doing something I don't usually do, write a review without using the service. This clinic was one of the 3 clinics that my private health insurance authorised, so it was my preferred one, but they just don't answer the phone, it's …

My experience at Lister Fertility Clinic was one of repeated disappointment, mismanagement, and emotional distress—this review only touches the surface.

Despite giving the clinic multiple chances, I was repeatedly let down.
It’s deeply upsetting to admit how vulnerable I was—trusting them not once, but three times. That’s my mistake to own, but no patient should be left feeling foolish for placing trust in a medical team.

While I do appreciate Mr. Salim’s expertise, patient care - it becomes distressingly irrelevant when the wider team fails so profoundly.

The embryology team was shockingly unempathetic—conversations felt cold and transactional, with no recognition of the emotional weight involved.

I was issued the wrong prescription, and only avoided wasting more time, risking my health, and incurring further financial harm because I caught the error myself.

One nurse outright refused to write down my protocol during our consultation and dismissed me rudely.

By the time the team finally followed up, my original sperm donor had sold out. I could have left the clinic then—but gave them the benefit of the doubt. I was later offered a replacement donor that required ICSI by the third cycle, only to discover—after the fact—that the donor company used by Lister includes a disclaimer stating they are not responsible if the sample fails to meet advertised motility. The clinic itself wasn’t even aware of this at the time and had to chase the information.
They offered a compensatory amount that was effectively cancelled out by rising costs—a complete mockery of my financial and emotional losses.

This level of risk and ambiguity was never explained to me before making such a significant decision. Only to be brushed off , that ICSI is to my advantage.. again another inconsistency , when initially I was told no avoid ICSI!

Further inconsistencies - At my first consultation, I was told IUI wasn’t a feasible option for me —only to later be advised to pursue it after three failed IVF cycles. At which point is there any consideration of the impact of the suggestion ? None. Sadly. None!

I was also charged for injections I didn’t receive, without any prior explanation or warning. After a miscarriage, I was left paying for medication I no longer needed, and was told by a nurse to “just use it for your next one”—completely ignoring what was physically or emotionally possible, and well beyond her role to advise.

It took over four weeks to receive my clinic notes, and only after my gynaecologist’s team chased them. Otherwise, I would have faced even longer delays. I was promised a call from a quality manager, only to be dismissively asked, “Didn’t you get the email?”—no follow-up, no courtesy, no accountability.

It’s clear that some patients at this private clinic are treated as financial numbers—not people. The only positive interactions I had were with the counsellor, who ended up supporting me more through the pain caused by the clinic than through the IVF itself, and one senior nurse, who was kind and empathetic—a rare exception.

If you value clarity, consistency, dignity, or even basic manners, please don’t come here.

The only thing this clinic has consistently delivered is the swift convincing and withdrawal of funds.

Proceed with caution—this is only the tip of the iceberg.

It’s deeply humiliating to share something so personal, knowing people I know may see it—but I’m doing so in the hope that it might help someone else to avoid this kind and depth of trauma.

SCAMMERS!!! Booked an initial consultation for IVF and not once got told about having to pay £250! For 15 minutes that told us nothing, consultant asked us to send an email and then had a go because we were moving the phone, sending said email she asked us to send, phone lines go unanswered and no-one wants to help as "it's not my department", avoid at all costs unless you like your money going to waste!

Absolutely terrible. It has taken me two years to recover emotionally when I ought to have done a second cycle there an then. I couldn't bear it because of the emotional trauma experienced by me owing to the complete and utter lack of professionalism from this clinic.

I had planned my entire life for my December cycle (time off work, support from my sister who also booked time off work). I bought the drugs, I had all the nurses appointments and the initial doctor's consultantion and not once did anyone tell me that the clinic closes over the Christmas period. I found out by chance. This completely supported my plans and if I didn't stumble upon the information, I would have proceeded with injections, turned up for scans and retrieval only to find the clinic and the hospital closed.

I made a formal complaint to which their response was that they found no wrong doing. I guess this is typical of a situation like this because of course their staff would have lied during investigations.

Anyway, I had already paid so I had to do it with them two months later. Retrieved less eggs than predicted. Obviously medically can't be proven but I believe this is connected to the stress the clinic caused me. They wouldn't even give my money back so I could consider elsewhere. They agreed that the remainder of my communication would be with a doctor of my choosing. I chose the Dr I had for my initial consultation who was fine to begin with. But after retrieving my eggs, didn't even bother to come and see me in person in the hospital room, just a 30second phone to tell me how many eggs were retrieved.

My experience of this place is that this is a factory farm for egg freezing etc. No one actually cares about the women and people in front of them.

I actually hate these people for how they made me feel.

This clinic is actually awful. I went in for a general check-up. I have private insurance and provided my authorization code at the appointment, and they assured me all was fine. Since then, I’ve been receiving letters claiming I have an …

They apply the same plan to everyone without giving specific attention to individual needs. Initially, they created a plan for me, but on the start date, I noticed that the injection dosages were different from what they had originally told me. I had to call them to confirm which dosage was correct. When I reached out, their response was dismissive: "It's okay, it doesn't make a difference. Let's just follow whatever you suggest!" It felt like I was left to manage my own medication. Throughout the process, there were so many errors which showed no coordination between teams. They kept asking me about my medication, my plan as if I was the doctor. I constantly tried to reach a doctor, but it was nearly impossible to speak with anyone. The entire experience felt like being part of a factory, with no attention given to individual needs. The planning was also very poor. Additionally, they had assured me that the initial pregnancy scan was included in the package, but later, I was charged for it. Unfortunately for the money it charges, I would definitely not recommend this place....

Not Recommended. Their patient care is below substandard - you see a different  doctor at each appointment so there is absolutely no point in choosing your doctor.  As a result some random nurse or doctor who has never seen your medical …

This is the clinic with the highest number of consultants and nurses I've  ever seen and the less efficient ... a big disappointment. During your cycle you will see at every appointment a different doctor or nurse. Repeating same conversations over and over again with each doctor since they don't take any notes. They are rushing all the time and to speak to a doctor you have to wait hours. Literally a doctor that never seen you before will decide your next step after having a quick look at your file.
Be prepared to take a lot of time off from work since early appointments are very rare.
The lack of monitoring is seriously worrying I had many cancelled cycles here comparing to other clinics.
They have a standard blood test/scan system that use for every patient, nothing is personalized. They are not worried if you waste your money.

On my first cycle my consultant couldn't remember what protocol she put me on. First blood test on day 7.. that was a cancelled cycle... and the second too.
Witnessed a lady going for embryo transfer and cancel  because embryologist mixed fresh with frozen embryos.
On another occasion one of the consultants told me the full bladder was needed for me to see the operation on the screen!!! Yes really...
They almost never reply to emails and some doctors are very rude and don't like to be questioned or tell you to go somewhere else.
Prices for blood tests are the highest sometimes double compared to other clinics.
The positive side they have some lovely nurses and a free counselling service and after a few months at the Lister you will need it.

Two different consultants advised me of two different protocols and contradicted each other. I was out on the ‘short’ protocol and at my follow up
was told that that doctor had ‘retired’ and that they wouldn’t have put me on short protocol.
I wasn’t given any advice or treatment about other considerations such as thyroid, Vitamin D, let alone fluid and protein intake.
I got 2 day 5 blastocysts - but after changing clinics I got 5 day 5 blastocysts and 3 day 6 blastocysts at my new clinic.

The admin team chased me for payment on frozen embryo storage, after I had left the clinic and didn’t have any embryos left there! When I queried it they had to get the embryology team to double check!!!
Very unprofessional and mostly unfriendly nurses including one who had long fake nails and didn’t wash hands before taking bloods.

All. I write to advise you to avoid Lister . avoid the head consultant James Nicopoullos. avoid Dr Marie Wren. But most importantly avoid Lister ! You need professional , attentive , consistent and premium treatment during ivf and our experience will show Lister do not care to provide this . We received alarming test results which were “the lowest reading we had ever received” - but they failed to act on these paid for tests resulting in wasted embryos . Avoid ! Avoid ! Avoid !

My partner and I went to The Evewell Clinic for secondary infertility and, now that our journey has ended, I feel compelled to share our mixed experience. We entered IVF knowing nothing and were completely unprepared for how painful and overwhelming it would be.

The nursing team was the highlight of the clinic—kind, patient, and genuinely supportive. They explained things clearly and offered emotional reassurance when we needed it most. Unfortunately, this level of care didn’t extend to the rest of the clinic. The administrative and finance staff often lacked empathy and organisation, and their communication felt cold and dismissive at times. For a process as emotionally heavy as IVF, this really mattered.

Dr. Eva was pleasant, but difficult to reach, and important information wasn’t communicated clearly. We often felt left in the dark, especially about risks, statistics, and what to expect. Messages went through a central team, and replies were slow or never came. This added a lot of unnecessary stress.

Costs were another major issue. We repeatedly asked for clarity but were only given a price list full of jargon we didn’t understand. No one explained what an average patient might expect to pay. We also later discovered we could have bought medication far cheaper elsewhere—something the clinic never mentioned, even though the difference was over £1,000 in our case. It left us feeling taken advantage of at a time when we were vulnerable.

Across two IVF cycles, we created 13 embryos and ended up with two viable ones after PGTA testing. Our first transfer failed. The second embryo was lost during thawing—something with a 99% success rate. We were devastated and unconvinced by the vague explanation of “it happens sometimes.” We are still convinced it was human error. After that, they suggested ovulation induction, which was poorly managed and far more expensive than implied.

What hurt most was the lack of guidance. We weren’t told early on that, at our age, donor eggs might be a realistic consideration—information that could have helped us prepare emotionally. They only discussed it after our second failure, and even admitted they lose patients because they don’t offer donor services. It felt like information was withheld simply because it didn’t benefit the clinic.

By the end, we felt abandoned. No aftercare, no follow‑up, no support—just silence. Not even a sales pitch. After everything we went through, that was incredibly painful.

I’m sure there are better clinics out there, and I hope others have a kinder, more transparent experience than we did.

Given this is a private clinic my expectations for after care was much higher.

There are much more professional clinics in London where you can get fertility treatment and receive much better care. I wish I had used one of them and not had the experience I have had with LWC.

Update:

I found the clinic through doing research and trusted them because of this and the fact that they are on Harley Street and you’d expect a better service from a clinic on “Harley street”

The biggest mistake I made was going to this clinic. On the initial consultation i told the nurse Georgina that I was adamant doing the procedure in March, because this was the only month I could do it( I travel a lot for work). However, I had a business trip when I was meant to get my period and mentioned to Georgina I would need to delay the period in order to start the process when I returned. I was told this was no problem and that I can take period blockers which would delay my period and I could start the egg freezing process when I was back.

I was given a prescription for norethisterone and told to take it once a day. This is what
I did - I was also told by a friend that she was on the same pills and was told to take 3 times a day, which confused me and I called the clinic and asked my coordinator Orla to confirm how many times a day I should be taking the medication, to which she said once a day.

Unfortunately, on the 24th of February I got my period, and when I checked the box of the medication it said I was meant to be taking the medication 3 times a day. When I called the clinic up I had to wait to then find out that they made a mistake and I was meant to be taking 2-3 pills a day. As you can imagine I was very upset and reminded them that this was the only month I could have done this procedure. At this point, I was put in contact with a doctor from the clinic, who ensured me that I could still go ahead with the egg freezing, even though my period started. The nurse said that she did treatment on people that don’t even get a period or patients that have cancer. Being compared to a patient with cancer doesn’t
Make me feel good. I am healthy and young I should not be compared to a patient with cancer.

To cut the story short, I could not do the treatment when I wanted to, as it was too late. I wasted money and time to attend all my appointments to then be misinformed about how to take medication. You would expect a fertility clinic to know the correct dosage. Do not compare your patients to others, educate your employees and stop taking advantage. Do not choose this clinic. They do not know what they are doing. All they care about is money, the communication is terrible. They are more expensive than other clinics. They will leave you in the dark and they only time you will have good communication is when they are taking payment from you.

They say they care about mental health but they do not, I am now not able to do the egg freezing until the end of the year, as I do not have the time. I made this all very clear when I first contacted the clinic. I was told “sorry it is human error”. I do not expect this human error from a “leading” woman’s clinic in Harley Street. This clinic chargers more than others, I would advice not choosing this clinic if you want to be taken care of, look elsewhere, into a smaller a smaller clinic that value their patients more. At this clinic you are just a number, they want your money and they don’t even know the ins and outs of treatment. I ended up having to google how many times a day I should be taking medication instead of being informed from either the nurse or my co ordinator.

I hope no other woman will have to go through what I went through.

I would describe LWC as a poor, substandard and lazy medical institution. The comments below are my honestly held, personal opinions based on my experience having IVF at the LWC in 2023. I could not go into the detail I wanted due to limited word count unfortunately.

I am 34 and had an admittedly difficult diagnosis of very low AMH, which was weaponised to repeatedly tell me that IVF would be a hopeless endeavour and strongly push towards [the much more expensive] egg donation route. The IVF protocol I was put on was incredibly basic - I felt there was a lack of any personalised treatment which could have better served me and my diagnosis.

There is no routine monitoring of blood work at all throughout an IVF cycle at LWC; this is truly baffling when considering IVF is a hormonal based treatment and the only way to assess its efficacy is to consistently monitor blood hormone levels. I found this to be lazy and an excuse to do the bare minimum.

I had one dominant follicle which burst. LWC brushed this off as "bad luck" as opposed to considering the impact of the extremely high stimulation I was placed on coupled with the lack of any proper monitoring blood work. In a meeting just after this news was broken to me, I was treated insensitively; I felt the doctor used this cycle failure to justify his own prediction of a poor outcome, as opposed to reflecting as to how a better outcome could be achieved in the future. I found this to be highly disappointing and again, lazy. I was again pushed to switch to egg donation.

I was then ignored and forgotten about - literally. I was promised a revised treatment plan and I heard nothing back. After 25 days, I contacted LWC to ask what was happening. I had simply been forgotten. This behaviour does not strike me as a clinic who truly cares about its patients. When the treatment plan arrived, it was extremely basic and contained no information as to how it would remedy the previous failures. I found this to be lazy and highly indicative of poor patient care.

The administrative side of LWC was chaotic; it adds rather than detracts from the stress you are under. Letters were sent after many weeks and only after chasing - I felt they were curt, poorly written and contained limited helpful medical advice. Paperwork was regularly not in order.

I felt something was not right, the doctor had no faith in me, he seemed unmotivated in helping me achieve a successful outcome - seemingly contented with his diagnosis of me being bound to fail. The doctor also strongly suggested that no other clinic would be able to achieve success either, telling me verbatim: "if anyone tells you that they can collect more than one egg from you they are lying".

Despite the LWC's indication that I was a hopeless case which no fertility clinic could help, I decided to seek a second opinion. After 2 rounds of IVF at another clinic, we made 6 embryos and I am now 4.5 months pregnant. The LWC's dogmatic and rigid assessment that I would was an unsuitable candidate for IVF was simply medically incorrect. Had I stayed at LWC, I would have never had the chance to have my own children - and they sought to convince me this was an inevitability.

I formally complained to the LWC; they made partial admissions to "poor communication" but nothing further. My complaint was poorly handled, with many points I raised being ignored. Before I escalated the complaint to the regulator, I provided the LWC with a further chance to reply more fully as a courtesy - they declined. This struck me as a business who did not care what their patients thought of them, nor what the regulator would think of them.

LWC not only took our hard earned life savings, but something much, much more valuable - our time. I felt denigrated and unsupported during one of the most vulnerable times of my life.

Please, please carefully consider whether this clinic is for you - particularly if you have a more complex infertility diagnosis. You deserve to have the best treatment possible during what is probably one of the most difficult times of your life.

I spent a long time thinking about whether to write this review, and then I realised - some people only have one chance and one pot of money...
My Story: I began my fertility journey at LWC London Bridge. Everything seemed fine at first - I started the injections, followed the protocol, and was preparing for egg collection. But the day before the procedure, a scan revealed that my eggs had already started releasing. This meant no eggs could be collected.
Later, I learned that this could have been prevented with frequent blood tests to monitor my hormone levels - something LWC didn’t do. Instead, I was told this happens to “1 in 100 people.” But had I been monitored properly, it likely wouldn’t have happened at all. The cost for this round set us back approx £6k, it felt difficult at the time too, we had to wait weeks to go again.
Round Two: Determined to try again, I went ahead with a second round at LWC. This time, the doctors were understandably cautious, but it resulted in only seven eggs collected, leading to two low-grade embryos and no baby. By this point, I had spent approx £18,000, and I was left feeling like my egg reserve was low and my body wasn’t responding well.
While the nurses at LWC are lovely and genuinely want the best for patients, the monitoring simply wasn’t tailored or thorough enough.
The Turning Point: We done some research and made the decision to switch to ARGC, (this clinic has one of highest success rates in the UK I believe alongside the Evewell clinic) . On my first round there, 23 eggs were collected - yep, 23. In hindsight, my struggles at LWC weren’t due to poor egg reserve or my body not responding. The problem was inadequate monitoring. With closer tracking, including more frequent blood tests, my body responded just fine. Thanks to the other clinic we now have a beautiful baby and 5 good grade embryos in the freezer.
My Advice: If you have unlimited resources and can afford multiple rounds, LWC may be an option. But if you have only one shot - do your research. Ask to see their success rates. In my experience, they are lower than other clinics, and proper monitoring could make all the difference in your journey. I’m sorry LWC for having to write this review, however this is the truth, and people struggling to have a baby or start a family haven’t got endless pots of money, I hope you can understand.

This clinic was one of the worst experiences a young woman could have. At 33, I went in for a fertility test ahead of egg freezing. I was met with a poor result (fair enough) and how the doctor handled it was in a “poor you” patronizing …

We are deeply dissatisfied with some parts of the treatment process. Following our initial consultation there was no further information mentioned to us about the Fibroid that was found in the initial scan, we were informed that we would be updated if there were any concerns or any further procedures that would need to be considered or happen as part of IVF treatment.

Then I was told to go into the clinic for the embryo transfer, we were told this would be a quick procedure and my partner wouldn’t need me to accompany me.

We then received a message on the app that partner should accompany and was then told that we should have been informed this previously,however it was too late for my partner to be able to attend by this point.

At the embryo transfer appointment I was then told that I can have an option to remove Fibroid which may increase chances of pregnancy, but this would include further costs, time, and potential risks of freezing embryos. This had not been mentioned to us at all since the initial consultation and was a shock to be given a choice to make on the day of the embryo. transfer that I was not expecting at all.   I was informed that I had to make the decision on the same day.

This was extremely stressful situation to be in without any prior conversations about this at all since the initial consultation. This was very distressing decision to make at this point.

I was also told that this wasn’t managed earlier as staff didn’t expect the embryos to be healthy or good. This again is extremely upsetting to hear on the day of the embryo transfer, and we are dissatisfied that this was not discussed with us previously.

I could’ve arranged the Fibroid procedure at any time since our initial consultation to not cause so much distress and challenging decision to make on the day of the embryo transfer.

For the money that we spent, £9000 we are really dissatisfied with this private service and expected better care on this journey.

When we complained they replied that It was unclear if we had been told this information after we had informed them that we hadn’t. We wouldn’t trust this clinic anymore and have gone somewhere else for further treatment.

Urgent!!! PLEASE AVOID THIS CLINIC I've applied to be an egg donor for altruistic reasons. I wasn't aware the clinic makes a fortune selling donated eggs but well, it's a private clinic after all. I was greeted by the doctor who hugged me, told me what wonderful support they would give me and what an amazing person I was to be a donor. Fast forward to the scan - turns out one of my ovaries is really small and I cannot be a donor cause the clinic cannot squeeze enough eggs out of me to make good business. This is where the pleasant phase ended. I will never forget the disgust in doctors eyes when she looked at me. I was pretty much kicked out on the street with a promo leaflet saying I should freeze my remaining eggs for 25K £. Needless to say I was devastated emotionally as I never worried about my fertility before. Greedy despicable people. I went for a private scan today to a different clinic, everything is fine with my ovaries. I just wasn't a cash cow for the Bridge Centre. If you're reading this I'm sure you are desperate to have a baby. Please choose a different clinic that actually respects people and doesn't abuse egg donors.

Avoid. I chose the clinic mainly based on location disregarding mixed reviews I came across while doing my research. DOB on the forms were incorrect and we were not able to update it ourselves. My husband asked reception to update DOB after his appointment. When I came for my appointment I was told that it was my date that was incorrect which is not the case. I kindly asked to update DOB on the forms, given we have signed them, notified them, they have scans of our ID documents and we have physically been in the premises already. You would think it's an easy thing to do? No. I receive another email asking to complete the forms (no explanation why and what needs to be completed) when I question a different person who came back basically saying the same thing (no explanation what has happened, what i need to do and still with the wrong DOB). After further questioning why the exercise needs to be repeated, I received a call from an enquiry person who clearly didn't familiarise herself with the issue before calling me. Eventually the outcome was that forms have been lost, there is nothing they can do and if we want to have consultation we need to yet again complete the forms. Private clinics dealing with people who are already going through difficult processes should have shown more professionalism and empathy instead of wasting patients' time. At the very least have an efficient process ensuring at least the basic things are working properly. Losing data is a breach of GDPR and I should be informing the Information Commissioner's Office.

The tests went quickly and there was no waiting time to be seen. Reception was nice but treatment rooms v basic (typical NHS standard). I suggest taking your money somewhere else if you can. Not worth the hassle and even more upset :(

AVOID THIS CLINIC!! Please please please take the time to research clinics and read through the many 1 star reviews this company has received, you will notice common complaints and they are true, from experience I am sad to say. On top of all of that, their advertising is totally misleading, SO EXPENSIVE AND SO MANY HIDDEN COSTS! DO NOT WASTE YOUR MONEY HERE.

We've had our IUI treatment in this centre. It's amazing how unprofessionally it has been all handled. I arrived for one of the scans to see if i am ready for the treatment and the head nurse said that they've missed my ovulation and it would be a waste of money to have this done that day so i would have to try to have the treatment next month instead. i was quite upset considering this is something the other centres normally control monitoring their patients closely. I went home and got a call from a nurse later that day saying that doctor thinks its still a good time to have the IUI done. Even though i was doubtful about that because the information from the Head Nurse was very different in a morning, i still had it done that day around 5pm (which is also not recommended by professional fertility centres, it is normally done in a morning or early afternoon).
The treatment was unsuccessful. In a month time we went to see the doctor recommended by our friends (in a different centre). He explained how the IUI treatment is normally done and we were shocked! Bridge centre failed to do the absolute standard procedure: no injection to prevent to ovulate too early, no trigger injection, they didn't even tell me to have a rubella vaccination before the treatment which is necessary in this country if you ever had one (and i even had the test done so they were very well aware of it). Wasted 3000 pounds in total. I wish i never had this experience with the Bridge centre.

I would be careful using this clinic . I was there was egg freezing and  made to feel I was being convinced I had something which subsequent and expensive tests have proved I didn't have. I also had to chase for the result. Subsequent Drs were shocked I’d even been tested given the unlikelihood .
It is important to treat people as individuals.
If women are going through this emotional experience then a greater degree of sensitivity is required . Another review said cold and I would agree this was my experience.
Also on a side note I was told conception would  be difficult in my case and I’d need expensive drugs. Currently 25 weeks pregnant with my first child at 39 (took two gos).
